_description: Rules for triggering FlowManager flows from XUI objects using root-level _flow and optional _flow_event.
_core_rules: Use root-level _flow on XUI objects to trigger FlowManager flows.
Do NOT use _on.click._op:"fire" to run FlowManager flows.
Use _on/_op:"fire" only for raw XEM events.
Use _flow_event to change the trigger event.
_flow can be a string flow id or an object with _id and _payload.
_payload values are passed to the flow event payload.
Do NOT set _flow_auto:false unless explicitly requested.
Do NOT nest _flow under _on, _on.click, or any event handler.
Do NOT generate JavaScript handlers, DOM onclick, addEventListener, React, or Vue syntax.
_priority_rules: When generating flow-triggering buttons, NEVER generate _on.click._op:"fire".
If a button runs a flow, use root-level _flow only.
Do NOT combine _flow with _on.click fire patterns.
Do NOT generate _flow_auto:false unless the user explicitly asks to disable automatic flow binding.
fire nano command is for raw XEM events, not FlowManager flows.
For click-triggered flows, omit _flow_event unless a non-click event is required.
For input/change-triggered flows, set _flow_event:"change" or another explicit event.
When passing UI event values to a flow, use _flow as an object with _id and _payload.

_fields._flow: Root-level XUIObject field. Runs a FlowManager flow. Can be a string flow id or an object with _id and _payload.
_fields._flow_event: Optional root-level field. DOM event name used to trigger the flow. Defaults to click.
_fields._flow_auto: Optional root-level field. Set false to prevent automatic flow binding.
_fields._payload: When _flow is an object, _payload defines values passed to the flow event payload.

_patterns._description: Input change runs a flow and passes event value.
Button click runs a flow with static or dynamic payload.

_anti_patterns._reason: This fires a raw XEM event. FlowManager flows must use root-level _flow.
_flow is a root-level XUIObject field, not nested under _on.click.
This is a flow definition shape. XUI flow triggers should reference a flow id or use _flow._id with _payload.

_notes: XUI objects trigger flows through root-level _flow.
Do not use _on.click.fire for FlowManager flows.
Use _on.click.fire only for raw XEM signals.
